title: Inventory mycotoxin test data, climate suitability models, and post-harvest practice surveys for SSA aflatoxin risk mapping
outcome: A dated source inventory that separates mycotoxin contamination test data (market surveys, household samples, regulatory monitoring), climate suitability models for Aspergillus flavus, post-harvest practice surveys, and population dietary exposure data into usable, limited, and rejected categories with explicit country-level coverage assessment.
inputs: IARC and WHO aflatoxin hazard assessments
Published mycotoxin contamination surveys from SSA countries
FAO and national food safety authority monitoring data
Climate reanalysis and Aspergillus suitability model documentation
DHS and national nutrition survey dietary recall modules for staple consumption patterns
expected_artifact: datasets.md update plus evidence.json records for each accepted source.
validation_method: Reviewer checks each source for URL, date, access status, test method or model documentation, geographic grain, crop type coverage, sampling frame, and stated limitations.
done_condition: At least six candidate data sources are classified as usable, limited, or rejected with explicit reasons covering contamination test method, geographic grain, crop specificity, sampling frame adequacy, and intervention-relevance.

title: Compile district-level aflatoxin contamination test results for one to three SSA countries
outcome: A cleaned contamination dataset for one to three selected countries with documented grain, test method, detection limit, sampling frame, district identifier, collection date, and known missingness patterns.
inputs: national food safety authority monitoring records where accessible
expected_artifact: datasets.md and validation.md updates with compiled contamination data inventory and data quality rules.
validation_method: Replicator reruns the data compilation on the documented sources and confirms grain, test method consistency, missingness handling, and district identifier matching.
done_condition: Every contamination data point has a documented test method, detection limit, grain type, sampling frame, district identifier, collection date, and missingness flag, and at least one country has enough data density to attempt sub-national risk mapping.

title: Review climate and crop-stress variables driving Aspergillus infection risk
outcome: A ranked list of candidate climate and crop-stress variables (temperature, humidity, drought stress, insect damage, rainfall timing) with hypothesized lead-time and known data availability for SSA sub-national analysis.
inputs: crop calendar data for maize and groundnut growing seasons
expected_artifact: task-map.md and validation.md updates with candidate variable tests.
validation_method: Back-test candidate variables against historical contamination survey data and report which variables show spatial or temporal correlation with measured contamination.
done_condition: No candidate climate variable is accepted as a risk predictor without back-test results, limitations, and replication status against at least one country's contamination data.

title: Review biocontrol and post-harvest intervention evidence with failure modes and scaling constraints
outcome: A structured review of Aflasafe biocontrol, improved drying practices, hermetic storage, and sorting interventions with efficacy range, cost, scaling requirements, and documented failure modes in smallholder contexts.
inputs: post-harvest technology intervention studies from SSA
expected_artifact: outputs.md and validation.md updates with intervention evidence summary and allocation decision framework.
validation_method: Reviewer checks that each intervention has documented efficacy, cost, scaling requirement, failure mode, and a named decision-maker. A second reviewer replicates the evidence-strength assessment independently.
done_condition: Every intervention recommendation names the decision-maker, the allocation it would change, the evidence strength, and at least one failure mode that could make deployment ineffective or harmful, with replication of the evidence-strength assessment by a second reviewer.

title: Red-team how contamination risk maps could be misused for trade stigmatization, punitive inspection, or market exclusion
outcome: A failure-mode review identifying how district-level contamination risk maps could stigmatize regions, trigger unjustified trade restrictions, justify punitive inspection of smallholder farmers, or mislead consumers about local food safety.
expected_artifact: outputs.md and SAFETY.md linked review note.
validation_method: Reviewer confirms every high-risk misuse scenario has a mitigation or blocks publication, then a second reviewer replicates the failure-mode check.
done_condition: Every field-facing risk map output is either blocked, qualified with trade-protection warnings, or gated behind explicit safeguards against stigmatization and punitive use, with replicated red-team review before publication.

title: Check whether contamination risk maps match food safety authority and extension service decision needs
outcome: A field-facing review of whether the proposed risk map would change a real decision for a national food safety authority, extension service manager, or public health official without creating worse incentives.
expected_artifact: playbooks.md update with named use and non-use cases.
validation_method: Field reviewer identifies the actual decision, user, timing, required workflow, and misuse risk, then a second reviewer replicates the check on the stated non-use cases.
done_condition: The artifact has a named user, named decision, named intervention, explicit non-use cases, and replicated field-reality review before publication.
